title: Constrain and Name an Embedded Document
summary: An iframe creates a nested browsing context. Its title names the embedded content for assistive technology; loading can be deferred; sandbox and allow limit capabilities; and responsive sizing must prevent the nested document from breaking the parent layout. Predict, inspect, explain, and apply the contract in a cumulative public-information build.
objectives: Explain html iframe with source and browser evidence.
Apply html iframe without discarding earlier document work.
Diagnose the named misconception in a changed public-service scenario.

options.text: City meeting stream embed review case 1: an iframe automatically inherits the parent page language, permissions, and accessible name.
City meeting stream embed review case 1: the parent document must name the iframe, constrain its permissions, and make its box responsive.
City meeting stream embed review case 1: removing all constraints is safest because embedded content needs every browser capability.
